session_hint: the Kafka consumer group lag investigation
question: What did we conclude was causing the Kafka consumer group lag to grow unboundedly?
turns: The orders-processing consumer group on our Kafka cluster (v3.4) has been building lag since Wednesday. Topic is orders-v2, we have 12 consumer instances running. Lag is now 2.1M messages and climbing.
Checked consumer CPU and memory: all 12 instances healthy, each processing around 75 msg/s. Combined throughput is about 900 msg/s. Producer side is pushing 950 msg/s — barely treading water.
Looked at the topic config: orders-v2 has only 1 partition. Only one consumer in the group can ever read; the other 11 are sitting completely idle.

That's the root cause. With a single partition we're capped at one consumer's throughput regardless of how many instances we run. Increased to 16 partitions and lag drained to zero in under 30 minutes.
